messages.content:
Hello!

I would like to report an issue with the [Hard dataset](https://huggingface.co/datasets/SAIRfoundation/equational-theories-selected-problems/blob/main/data/hard.jsonl): only 69 out of 200 entries form unique `(equation1, equation2)` pairs.

Here are a few dupe examples ([see full list file, one row per unique equation pair](/user_uploads/5/c7/jglNSkwuY-W5aFFJbwylvOoq/hard_problems_unique_pairs.txt)):
- hard_0024, hard_0076, hard_0128, hard_0180
- hard_0047, hard_0099, hard_0151
- hard_0060, hard_0112, hard_0164
- hard_0020, hard_0072, hard_0124, hard_0176
- hard_0031, hard_0083, hard_0135, hard_0187

NB: no such finding for the "Normal" dataset
@**Baptiste Polchlopek** Thank you for reporting the issue. We are looking into it.
Oops, it appears we did not code a certain random number generator correctly. We have generated a fresh set of 200 "hard" implications and will deploy these shortly after some testing.

We have uploaded two new public problem subsets to Hugging Face:

* hard2 is now available as a new 200-problem subset. It contains 100 problems with ground-truth TRUE labels and 100 problems with ground-truth FALSE labels.
 Link: https://huggingface.co/datasets/SAIRfoundation/equational-theories-selected-problems/viewer/hard2/train
 
* hard1 is also now available as the deduplicated version of the previous hard subset. It contains 69 problems total, with 24 ground-truth TRUE labels and 45 ground-truth FALSE labels.
 Link: https://huggingface.co/datasets/SAIRfoundation/equational-theories-selected-problems/viewer/hard1/train

Both subsets are now publicly available.

Thanks to @**Baptiste Polchlopek** for pointing out the issue.
1. Can assume the playground @ sair foundation 200 hard problems updated as well?

hard1.jsonl (69 problems) is the deduped version of the previous 200 hard problems. 
hard2.jsonl is a new hard problem set with 200 new hard problems
You can use both. Thanks!
Wonderful I see them both there now tyvm
